issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on blockquote
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote element uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum) for normal text which requires 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 303: `color: var(--colour-neutral-600);` (or `color: #595959;`)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus state sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 596: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector inherits outline: none from the base rule and only changes outline-color. Without outline-style and outline-width, the focus ring remains invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 361: `.focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it for screen readers. It also lacks the necessary clip or clip-path properties.
issuesFound.fix: Line 371-380: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, which can cause motion animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transition for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width of 28px and height of 28px with zero padding, resulting in a 28×28 px touch target. This is smaller than the recommended 44×44 px (WCAG 2.5.8) and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator relies on box-shadow on #search-input:focus

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content

issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum (and 44px recommended) for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px. While above the 24px absolute minimum, this is below the 44px recommended size and may be difficult to activate on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually for screen readers only. This breaks the intended accessibility pattern.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation, but the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block (Line 529) is empty and does not disable the motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transform transition for hover/active states, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img uses transform transition, but the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block (Line 1009) is empty and does not disable the motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .feature-card p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on blockquote
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote element uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1).

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the default focus outline via outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border-color change in the same rule.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can significantly impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu link focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links ([id$="-submenu"] li a) remove the focus outline with outline: none and provide only outline-offset: -3px, which renders no visible focus ring for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible to sighted users and fails to hide it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technology. It should use position: absolute with 1px dimensions and clipping.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, placing it in the 24–43px range without the recommended 8px padding compensation to achieve the 44px target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only button class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size and potentially causing activation failures for users with tremors or limited precision.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set min-height: 24px with no visible padding compensation in the rule, failing to meet the recommended 44px touch target size for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation link focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the default focus outline via outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search form submit button removes the default focus outline via outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border in the focus-visible state.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class removes the default focus outline via outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, failing to provide a visible focus ring for keyboard users in the focus-visible state.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .contact-section > p
issuesFound.explanation: The contact section description paragraph uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, failing WCAG AA contrast requirements.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size, making it difficult for users with motor disabilities to select.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button class includes transform in its transition property without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override, which can trigger vestibular disorders in users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition affecting transform properties (perspective rotation) without a reduced motion override, potentially causing discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses transform in its transition for the translateY animation without a prefers-reduced-motion override, which may cause issues for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button in the hero section includes transform in its transition without a reduced motion override, potentially triggering vestibular issues.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards use transform in their transition for the hover lift effect without a prefers-reduced-motion override, which can cause discomfort for motion-sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: Section header paragraphs use var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a white background, providing only 2.3:1 contrast ratio, which is below the 4.5:1 required for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .features-section .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The features section header paragraph uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background with insufficient contrast of approximately 2.3:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .product-body > p
issuesFound.explanation: Product card body text uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, failing to meet the 4.5:1 contrast ratio required for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block removes the focus outline entirely with outline: none and provides no visible alternative. In Windows High Contrast Mode, box-shadow is suppressed, so users receive no focus indication at all.
issuesFound.fix: Line 377: `outline: 3px solid ButtonText;` (replace `outline: none` inside the :focus-visible rule within the forced-colors media query)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible rule suppresses the default outline with outline: none and outline-offset alone, providing no visible replacement focus indicator for keyboard users navigating through buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 638: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can significantly impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities by making word boundaries harder to distinguish.
issuesFound.fix: Line 349: `body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links have their focus outline removed with outline: none and only outline-offset specified, leaving no visible focus ring for keyboard users navigating the submenu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 750: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. This defeats the purpose of screen-reader-only text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit dimensions of 28px by 28px with zero padding, which is below the 44px recommended size and has insufficient padding to compensate, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons have explicit dimensions of 20px by 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target requirement. With no compensating padding, these buttons are too small for reliable touch activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 690: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs have explicit dimensions of 20px by 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes them difficult to select accurately, especially for users with tremors or limited fine motor control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1243: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. An outline-offset applied to a zero-width outline renders nothing visible. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, blocking navigation entirely.

issuesFound.fix: Line 767: .btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); line-height: var(--line-height-tight); text-align: center; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; border: 2px solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); vertical-align: middle; position: relative; isolation: isolate; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .btn-lg
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-lg class has min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended size despite being a "large" variant.
issuesFound.fix: Line 826: .btn-lg { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-4) var(--space-8); font-size: var(--font-size-lg); border-radius: var(--radius-xl); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer nav links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but is below the 44px recommended size for touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1769: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links have min-height: 28px with padding-block: var(--space-2). The effective touch area may not reach 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1781: .social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto. This does NOT hide content visually - it leaves content fully visible on screen, defeating the purpose of screen-reader-only text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 608: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses words together. This impairs readability for users with dyslexia, low vision, or cognitive disabilities who rely on clear word separation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 288: body { word-spacing: 0em; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has letter-spacing: -0.05em, which compresses letters together. This impairs readability for users with dyslexia, low vision, or cognitive disabilities who need clear letter separation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 289: body { word-spacing: -0.2em; letter-spacing: 0em; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg) with transition on transform. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1089 exists but is empty, providing no override to disable the motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1089: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets text-decoration: none. The hover state only changes color (line 1786) without adding underline or other non-colour visual differentiator. Users who cannot perceive color differences cannot identify these as links.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1784: .social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 28px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }
  Line 1786: .social-links a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none on :focus-visible without replacement. In Windows High Contrast Mode, box-shadow is suppressed, so outline is the only reliable focus indicator. Removing it leaves users with no visible focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible selector
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users on all interactive elements that do not have specific overrides, making it impossible to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which is a negative value. This reduces space between words and can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 314: body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector only sets outline-offset: -3px but does not set an outline width or style, and relies on the global outline: none. The offset property has no visible effect when outline is none, leaving no focus indicator for elements using this class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 366: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have a transition property that includes transform, which can cause spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override that disables this transition for users who prefer reduced motion, which may trigger vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 388: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the touch target too small for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 767: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); ... }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector only sets outline-color but does not set an outline width or style, and inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule. No visible focus ring is rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 370: .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px with no explicit padding, resulting in a touch target of only 24px. This meets the minimum but falls short of the 44px recommended size, making navigation difficult for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1493: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) 0; ... }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, preventing keyboard users from seeing when the button is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 561: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links remove the outline without replacement, providing no visible indication of keyboard focus. This makes it impossible for keyboard users to track their position.
issuesFound.fix: Line 651: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.fix: Line 632: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.fix: Line 1480: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.explanation: Filter toggle buttons remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which filter is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual toggle buttons remove the focus outline without replacement, leaving keyboard users without visible focus indication.

issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, making keyboard navigation invisible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove the focus outline without replacement, preventing keyboard users from seeing their current position.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1497: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons set min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, resulting in a 28px touch target. While this meets the 24px minimum, it is below the 44px recommended size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 624: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and width/height: auto, which keeps the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 376: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: Skip links use transition: transform for their slide-in animation, but there is no reduced motion media query to disable this effect for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 388: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition property including transform that creates a 3D rotation effect. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 895 is empty and does not actually disable the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 895: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards have a transition including transform that causes them to lift on hover (translateY). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this spatial animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1108: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card has a transition including transform that causes scale and translate effects on hover. There is no reduced motion override for this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1299: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-danger
issuesFound.explanation: The danger button uses white text (#ffffff) on a light red background (#ee9999). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.1:1, which fails the 3:1 requirement for large text (bold 16px) and the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text. Users with low vision may not be able to read the button text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 754: --colour-error: #dc2626; (or darker) and Line 757: background-color: var(--colour-error);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .stat-label
issuesFound.explanation: The stat label uses rgba(255,255,255,0.3) (30% white) text on a rgba(255,255,255,0.08) background over a gradient. The resulting contrast is approximately 1.5:1, which is far below the required 4.5:1 for normal text. Users cannot read these labels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1192: .stat-label { color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .pricing-toggle button[aria-pressed="true"] .badge
issuesFound.explanation: The badge inside the active pricing toggle uses white text on a semi-transparent white background (rgba(255,255,255,0.25)), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.5:1. This fails the 3:1 requirement for the small bold text used in badges.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1284: .pricing-toggle button[aria-pressed="true"] .badge { background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); color: #ffffff; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .newsletter-section
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter section uses white text on a purple gradient background (#bb88ee). The contrast ratio between white and #bb88ee is approximately 2.5:1, which fails the 3:1 requirement for large text and 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision cannot read the content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1402: background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--colour-brand-primary) 0%, #7c3aed 100%); (using a darker purple)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on global a selector
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator such as border-bottom, font-weight increase, or background highlight. Links are only distinguished by colour, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 437: a { text-decoration: underline; } or add a { border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow in the same rule block. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all interactive elements across the site, making navigation impossible for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 337: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in high-contrast mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, :focus-visible sets outline: none. This ensures the focus ring is invisible specifically when users rely on high-contrast modes, blocking access for those with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 349: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has a fixed width and height of 28px with 0 padding, resulting in a touch target smaller than the recommended 44px. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 746: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; padding: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes are explicitly sized to 20px width and height, which is below the minimum 24px threshold. This makes them difficult to select accurately on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1369: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer links

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses a transform transition for its reveal animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 437: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which condenses text and reduces readability for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 229: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have transition properties including transform, which causes spatial movement, but no prefers-reduced-motion override exists to disable this for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img

issuesFound.fix: Line 659-661: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function toggles the mobile navigation drawer visible but never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2378: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu updates the visible panel and aria-expanded state, but does not announce the state change via a live region. When triggered by click, focus remains on the trigger button, leaving screen reader users unaware that content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2523 (in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` before the emit call. Line 2543 (in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ accordion item, the function animates the panel and updates state but fails to set the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true", leaving screen readers unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4130: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons toggle the active category but do not update their aria-pressed attribute to reflect the pressed state. The HTML contract expects aria-pressed, but the JavaScript only toggles CSS classes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3397: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop, immediately after the line calculating `const isActive = ...`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The helper function links an error message to a field via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1096: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` immediately after the null check line `if (!isElement(field) || !isElement(errorEl)) return;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function within the `mobileNav` module toggles the navigation drawer visibility by adding CSS classes but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers unaware that the menu is now open, as the `close` function correctly sets `aria-expanded="false"`, creating a state mismatch.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1330: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function in the `dropdownMenu` module reveals the dropdown panel by removing the `hidden` attribute but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The corresponding `_closeInstance` function sets `aria-expanded="false"`, causing the accessible state to remain false even when the menu is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1530: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the `faqAccordion` module expands the answer panel and updates internal state but omits setting `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger button. Since the `close` function explicitly sets `aria-expanded="false"`, assistive technologies will incorrectly report the accordion item as collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons use invalid aria-selected instead of aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing aria-expanded toggle

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clear

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function markInvalid updates aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. This causes validation errors to not be conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1070: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance and _closeInstance, but neither function updates aria-expanded on the trigger button (inst.trigger). Assistive technologies cannot track the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 1398: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visible content significantly, but no aria-live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1297: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`. Line 1318: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a FAQ item, the open() function adds CSS classes and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). The close() function sets it to false, creating an asymmetrical state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2462: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding and collapsing dropdown menus changes visible content but does not announce the state change via a live region. Users are not informed when a submenu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1387: add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`. Line 1405: add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` (or use appropriate strings from STRINGS).

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates visible prices across all plan cards, but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users may not realize prices have changed or what the new amounts are.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2277: add `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing\`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When initialising the mobile navigation, the trigger button (_trigger) does not have aria-expanded set to "false" initially. Screen readers assume an undefined state instead of knowing the menu is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1336: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_trigger.addEventListener('keydown', _onTriggerKeydown);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Applying filters updates the product grid and count badge, but does not announce the result count or filter status to screen readers. Users are not informed how many products match their selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2037: add `liveRegion.announce(\`\${totalMatches} products found\`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button controls the drawer element (_drawer), but aria-controls is never set on the trigger to programmatically associate it with the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1337: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error element from aria-describedby but does not remove or set to false the aria-invalid attribute. Fields remain marked as invalid after being corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1080: add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` after removing the describedby reference.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation for closed items
issuesFound.explanation: During initialisation, FAQ items that are closed (shouldBeOpen is false) do not have aria-expanded explicitly set to "false". Only open items get aria-expanded="true".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2532: add `else { setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); }` after the existing if block that sets it to 'true'.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded update in _onInputKeydown for Escape
issuesFound.explanation: When the user presses Escape to close the suggestion list, the code calls _close() but does not explicitly set aria-expanded="false" on the input (though _close should handle it, the asymmetry with the open path in _onInputKeydown where _open is called without setting aria-expanded is problematic).
issuesFound.fix: Ensure _close() sets aria-expanded="false" (Issue 4), and verify line 2178 after `_close();` that focus returns to input (which it does).

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, inlineValidator renders error messages and adds CSS classes but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Valid fields also do not have aria-invalid cleared to "false".
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5358: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` in the error state block (else branch) of _renderResult
  Line 5362: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` in the valid state block (if result.valid)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions show and hide the navigation drawer but never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2105: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`
  Line 2135: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function sets item.open = true and reveals the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4610: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but the _open and _close functions do not toggle aria-expanded to indicate when the suggestion listbox is displayed or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3208: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;` in _open
  Line 3225: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;` in _close

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation updates the visual UI but does not announce the state change ("Navigation opened" / "Navigation closed") via the live region system.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2112: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` inside open() after setting classes
  Line 2142: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` inside close() after emitting close event

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) in _syncToggleState only indicate active state via CSS classes. They lack aria-pressed, so screen readers cannot convey which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4155: Insert `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` inside the _periodBtns.forEach loop

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually but the billing period change is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4110: Insert `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);` after updating plan cards in setPeriod

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes visible content but does not announce the state change via a live region, relying solely on aria-expanded which may not convey content changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4620: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));` inside open() after removing hidden attribute
  Line 4660: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));` inside close() after setting hidden attribute

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens without trapping focus, allowing keyboard users to tab outside the drawer into the background content. The _trap variable is declared but never instantiated or activated.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2115: Insert `if (!_trap) _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true }); _trap.activate();` inside open() after _lockScroll()
  Line 2140: Insert `if (_trap) _trap.deactivate();` inside close() before _unlockScroll()

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels without announcing these visibility changes to screen readers, leaving users unaware that menu content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2358: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` at the end of _openInstance
  Line 2372: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` at the end of _closeInstance

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button scrolls the viewport but does not announce the action completion to screen readers, though focus is moved to the skip link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2760: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');` after the scrollToY call in _onClick

issuesFound.title: siteSearch selection missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a user selects a search suggestion, the panel closes and the input updates, but no announcement confirms the selection to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3260: Insert `liveRegion.announce(\`Selected \${item.label}\`);` at the start of _selectSuggestion function

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus return on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not programmatically returned to the hamburger trigger button that opened it, leaving keyboard users at the bottom of the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2145: Insert `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` at the end of close() function after _unlockScroll()

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing focus return on outside click
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdowns close due to a click outside, focus remains on the clicked element rather than returning to the trigger button that opened the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2455: Replace the _closeAll() call with: `const activeInst = _instances.find(i => i.open); const activeTrigger = activeInst?.trigger; _closeAll(); if (activeTrigger) activeTrigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on page load for mobileNav
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks aria-expanded="false" when the page loads and the menu is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2210: Insert `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside init() after _initialised = true

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized for dropdownMenu triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons registered via dropdownMenu.register() do not have aria-expanded="false" set on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2420: Insert `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside register() after setting aria-controls

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized for siteSearch input
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input lacks aria-expanded="false" initialization when the page loads.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3320: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside init() after mounting the listbox

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-controls pointing to the drawer element, preventing screen readers from identifying the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2215: Insert `if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));` inside init()

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on dropdown trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in the `dropdownMenu` module toggle the visibility of the panel using the `hidden` attribute and CSS classes, but they do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`inst.trigger`). Screen readers will not announce the state change (expanded/collapsed) to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2130: inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 2145: inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_open` and `_close` functions in the `siteSearch` module manage the visibility of the suggestion listbox but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the search input (`_inputEl`). This breaks the combobox pattern, preventing assistive technology from conveying the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3180: setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 3190: setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on FAQ trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the `faqAccordion` module expands the answer panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger (`item.trigger`). The `close` function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where screen readers may not announce the item as expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4320: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on mobile nav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the hamburger trigger button (`_trigger`). Users relying on screen readers will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2250: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 2270: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncToggleState` function in the `pricing` module toggles the `is-active` class on billing period buttons but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute. Assistive technology users are not informed which billing period (monthly/annual) is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3930: setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` function in `srUtils` links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid without this explicit state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1685: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid` function in `srUtils` removes the error description but does not remove or reset the `aria-invalid` attribute to "false". If the attribute was previously set, it remains stuck in the invalid state, misleading assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1697: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: Dynamic product count update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateCountBadge` function in `productFilter` updates the text content of the product count badge dynamically when filters change, but the element lacks an `aria-live` region and no announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the number of products has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3505: _countBadge.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite'); _countBadge.textContent = String(count);

issuesFound.title: Dynamic price update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updatePlanCard` function in the `pricing` module updates the price text content when the billing period changes, but no live region announcement is triggered. Users relying on screen readers are not notified that the price has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3855: plan.priceDisplay.textContent = target; liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target}`);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in markInvalid): add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled via CSS classes, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never updated to reflect the open or closed state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in open()): add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` Line (in close()): add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods dynamically updates visible prices across all plan cards, but no announcement is made to screen readers via the liveRegion utility. Users are unaware that prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after planDataParser.getAll().forEach(...)): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices updated to ' + period + ' billing');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands the FAQ answer panel and adds CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close() function sets it to false, the open() function never sets it to true, leaving screen readers unaware that the panel is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in open()): add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;` and before `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Filtering products updates the visible grid and count badge, but the result count is not announced to screen readers. Users do not know how many results match their selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside filterAnimation.run callback, after _updateCountBadge): add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Billing period buttons act as a toggle group (monthly vs annual) but only update CSS classes. They do not set aria-pressed to indicate which button is active, so screen readers cannot convey the selected state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside forEach): add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox trigger for the suggestion listbox, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" when the listbox is hidden on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');): add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes visible content but does not announce the state change. While focus remains on the trigger, the expanded/collapsed state change should be confirmed via live region for clarity.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in open()): add `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' expanded');` Line (in close()): add `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' collapsed');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is cleared or becomes valid, the function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. Screen readers may continue to treat the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in markValid): add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after updating aria-describedby references.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation drawer is a significant viewport change that should be announced to screen readers, especially since focus is not programmatically moved into the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in open()): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` Line (in close()): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus open and close without announcing their state change to screen readers, particularly when opened via hover or click.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _openInstance): add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` Line (in _closeInstance): add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer declares a focus trap variable (_trap) but never creates or activates it. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background content, which should be blocked when a modal drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after _lockScroll()): add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button does not have aria-controls linking it to the drawer panel it controls. Screen readers cannot identify which element is being expanded/collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after _initialised = true;): add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the navigation drawer visibility but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1330: function open(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the navigation drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1350: function close(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); ... }

issuesFound.fix: Line 1530: function _openInstance(inst) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function hides the panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The state change is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); ... }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger. Only the close function updates this attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2330: function open(item, announceToSR) { ... setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); ... }

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState does not update aria-pressed or aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function toggles the CSS active class on billing period buttons but does not update aria-pressed (for buttons) or aria-checked (for switch role). The selected state is not exposed to AT.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to indicate error state but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state of form fields using this component.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing button tracks whether monthly billing is selected via the value prop, but it lacks aria-pressed to communicate this state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1474: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing button tracks whether annual billing is selected via the value prop, but it lacks aria-pressed to communicate this state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1483: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby when an error exists, but the FormGroup wrapper is not provided with an errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage element is never rendered with that id. This creates a dangling reference that points to a non-existent element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1648: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}> Line 1654: Remove aria-describedby prop (FormGroup will handle the association)

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button manages the isOpen state to show or hide the answer panel, but it lacks aria-expanded. Assistive technology cannot announce whether the panel is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1584: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-controls={panelId} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter toggle buttons in FilterTabs indicate their active state via className and tabIndex but lack aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive which category is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1435: <button type="button" className={[ 'filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : '', ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slides missing aria-hidden on hidden items

'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to style the input as erroneous but does not map this to aria-invalid. Screen reader users cannot detect when the field contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 580: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the input element.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email field aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message element with id "newsletter-email-error" via aria-describedby when in error state, but FormGroup never renders an element with this id because the errorId prop is not passed. This creates a broken reference that prevents screen readers from announcing error messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1519: Ensure FormGroup receives errorId="newsletter-email-error" and errorMessage props, or remove the aria-describedby attribute.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter buttons act as a toggle group where one option is always active, but they lack aria-pressed. Screen readers announce them as plain buttons rather than toggle buttons, hiding the selected state from users.
issuesFound.fix: Line containing `<button` inside FilterTabs map: Add `aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value ? "true" : "false"}` to each button.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent disclosure trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ question button toggles the visibility of the answer panel (linked via aria-controls) but lacks aria-expanded. Assistive technology users cannot perceive whether the accordion item is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line containing `<button` in FaqItemComponent: Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` to the button props.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent ID
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error element ID 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby when in error state, but FormGroup is not passed an errorId prop, so no element with that ID exists in the DOM. This creates a broken ARIA reference and the error message is not announced by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line containing `<FormGroup` in NewsletterForm: Change prop `error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` to `errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` and add `errorId="newsletter-email-error"`.

issuesFound.title: Interactive div with onClick lacks role and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop div in MobileNav has an onClick handler to close the menu but lacks role="button", tabIndex, and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard users cannot activate it to close the menu, and it is not identified as interactive.
issuesFound.fix: Line 763: <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-hidden="true" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} />

issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: <nav aria-label="Mobile main" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection renders a section element without aria-labelledby or aria-label, despite containing a visible heading that could label the region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1400: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar renders a section element without aria-labelledby or aria-label, despite containing a visible heading that could label the region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1530: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard renders an article element containing a heading with a generated id, but the article lacks aria-labelledby to expose the heading as its accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1610: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: Section component ignoring labelledby prop
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a labelledby prop but does not apply it as aria-labelledby to the rendered element, preventing sections from being named by their headings.
issuesFound.fix: Line 990: <Tag id={id} aria-labelledby={labelledby} className={['page-section', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection CTAs group missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The div containing the primary call-to-action buttons is a group of related controls without an aria-label to describe its purpose to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1440: <div aria-label="Call to action links" className="hero-section__ctas">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection trust badges group missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The div containing trust badge elements is a group of related content without an aria-label to identify the region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1445: <div aria-label="Trust badges" className="hero-section__trust">

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader actions group missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The div containing the search, theme toggle, and CTA buttons is a group of related controls without an aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1360: <div aria-label="Header actions" className="site-header__actions">

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader CTAs group missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The div containing the Log in and Get started buttons is a group of related controls without an aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1370: <div aria-label="Header calls to action" className="site-header__ctas">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav footer CTAs group missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The div containing the mobile navigation footer buttons is a group of related controls without an aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: <div aria-label="Mobile navigation footer" className="mobile-nav__footer">

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The second duplicated list used for visual marquee looping is not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the integration list twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1520: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: StarRating individual stars not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters (★/☆) inside the StarRating component are decorative but lack aria-hidden, causing redundant announcements after the container's aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 905: {filled ? '★' : '☆'}

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element, potentially causing assistive technology to treat the overlay as the modal container.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1800: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}> and Line 1802: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown decorative chevron missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron span is purely decorative but is exposed to assistive technology, creating noise. It should be hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 642: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The link indicates the current page via className but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users are not informed this is the active page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 657: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop interactive div missing accessibility properties
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop div has an onClick handler but no role, tabIndex, or keyboard support. It is also behind a focus-trapped dialog, so it should be hidden from AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 725: <div aria-hidden="true" className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} />

issuesFound.title: MobileNav body nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: This nav region lacks an accessible name. Since there are multiple navs on the page (header, footer, mobile), each needs a unique label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 753: <nav aria-label="Mobile navigation" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The link indicates the current page via className but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users are not informed this is the active page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 779: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: This list is a visual duplicate for animation looping. Without aria-hidden, screen readers will announce the integration links twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1005: <ul aria-hidden="true" role="list" className="integrations-bar__list">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard CTA link missing accessible name with plan context
issuesFound.explanation: The link text is generic ("Start Pro free trial"). In a grid of plans, screen reader users need the plan name included in the accessible name to distinguish links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1243: {plan.ctaLabel}

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk is a visual indicator only. Without aria-hidden, screen readers will announce "star" or "asterisk" unnecessarily.
issuesFound.fix: Line 494: *

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup missing errorId causing broken aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby, but FormGroup does not receive errorId to assign to the ErrorMessage component. The ID will be undefined.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1595: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name FormGroup missing errorId causing broken aria-describedby

issuesFound.fix: Line 1660: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.fix: Line 1684: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" error={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message FormGroup missing errorId causing broken aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea references 'contact-message-error' via aria-describedby, but FormGroup does not receive errorId. The ID will be undefined.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1696: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} errorId="contact-message-error" error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: When isLoading is true, the button content changes to a spinner, but aria-busy is not set. Screen reader users are not informed the button is busy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 430: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading ? 'true' : undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>
